description_long: This traffic barricade features three horizontal wooden planks painted with alternating orange and white diagonal stripes, creating a highly visible warning pattern. The planks are mounted on a sturdy black metal A-frame structure, which provides stability and support. At the top of the barricade, there are two rounded finials, one on each side, adding a slight decorative touch and possibly serving as attachment points. The legs are wide-set and reinforced with a horizontal metal bar near the base, ensuring the barricade remains upright and difficult to tip over. The surface of the wood appears slightly textured, with a glossy finish that enhances visibility and weather resistance. The overall design is utilitarian, intended for temporary access control in urban environments such as streets, sidewalks, or construction zones.
description: A sturdy A-frame traffic barricade with orange and white diagonal stripes on three wooden planks, supported by a black metal frame and topped with rounded finials.
description_view_0: Front view showing three striped planks, black metal legs, and two rounded finials on top.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the A-frame structure and the thickness of the planks, with both sides of the barricade visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, but with the stripes mirrored and the same structural features visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the A-frame profile and the arrangement of the planks.
attribute_reflective_striping_pattern: Diagonal orange and white stripes, each stripe approximately equal width, repeating across all three planks.
